mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal a strengthening labor market, which drives higher aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive equity returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-GFC recovery narrative suggests labor market is a key focus for investors.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in PAYEMS shows a rank correlation below 0.05 with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate exceeds 5%, monetary policy is restrictive, increasing the discount rate for future earnings and tightening credit conditions for corporations and consumers, which negatively impacts equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Snapshot value of FEDFUNDS is 5.33, activating the trigger.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS is above 5% for at least three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales (RSAFS) and an increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a negative feedback loop between the labor market and consumer spending, signaling a deepening economic contraction and causing negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in RSAFS < 0 AND 3-month change in UNRATE > 0.2
evidence_refs: fact:GFC demonstrated the strong link between job losses and consumption cuts.
failure_condition: The combined signal does not produce a statistically significant negative mean 20-day forward return (p > 0.05) over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recessionary environment, an acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) confirms that the supply side of the economy is recovering, inventories are being rebuilt, and business investment is returning, leading to strong positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The decision date of Sept 2009 is in a post-shock recovery phase.
failure_condition: In the 24 months following a NBER-defined recession trough, the 6-month change in INDPRO is negatively correlated with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is low (below 4.5%) and begins to rise, it signals the end of the economic cycle and the beginning of a labor market downturn, leading to negative revisions of growth expectations and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE level < 4.5% AND 3-month change in UNRATE > 0.2
evidence_refs: fact:Snapshot UNRATE of 3.9 meets the level condition.
failure_condition: Triggered instances are not followed by negative median 20-day returns over two full business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts (HOUST) are a strong leading indicator of broad economic activity due to their high multiplier effect on construction, materials, and services, driving positive short-term equity market sentiment.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing sector weakness was central to the 2008 crisis; its recovery is a key signal.
failure_condition: The month-over-month change in HOUST has a negative rank correlation with 5-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve raises the FEDFUNDS rate while the labor market is simultaneously weakening (PAYEMS growth decelerates), it constitutes a policy error that risks triggering a recession, leading to sharply negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in FEDFUNDS > 0.25 AND 3-month change in PAYEMS < 1-year moving average
evidence_refs: fact:Historical examples of Fed tightening cycles ending in recession.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 has positive median 60-day returns in periods where the trigger condition is met for three or more consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A negative quarter-over-quarter growth rate in real GDP (GDPC1) is the technical definition of an economic contraction, directly impacting corporate revenue and earnings forecasts and causing a broad-based decline in equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:The GFC involved multiple quarters of negative GDP growth with severe market impact.
failure_condition: In walk-forward testing, the information coefficient of the signal is not statistically different from zero (p > 0.10) for 60-day forward returns.
